content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://doi.org/10.1002/pip.1025?urlappend=%3Futm_source%3Dresearchgate.net%26utm_medium%3Darticle", "source_title": "The effect of damp-heat and UV aging tests on the optical properties ...", "source_type": "other", "source_quality": 0.0, "overall_relevance": 0.0, "year": 0, "authors": [], "venue": "", "doi": "10.1002/pip.1025", "atomic_facts": [], "evidence_summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT: Cloudflare security verification page returned instead of article body. No extractable scientific content available from this source."}, {"source_url": "https://9021fdc6-0afa-473e-b60c-1dbccce51d7c.filesusr.com/ugd/84a06c_290bda798b6949ab879ead6b6b23f93b.pdf?index=true", "source_title": "Enhancement of resistance against damp heat aging through compositional change in PV encapsulant poly(ethylene-co-vinyl acetate)", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.72, "overall_relevance": 0.78, "year": 2020, "authors": [], "venue": "Photovoltaics Research", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "PEVA films with four different VA content levels (18, 24, 33, and 40%) were fabricated with necessary additives, cured at 150 degrees Celsius under vacuum, and then subjected to accelerated damp-heat aging at 85 degrees Celsius and 85% relative humidity according to IEC 61215 for durations of 1000 and 2000 hours.", "direct_quote": "PEVA films containing 18, 24, 33, and 40% VA content with the necessary additives have been fabricated and cured at 150 °C under vacuum and subsequently subjected to accelerated damp-heat (85◦C and 85% RH) aging according to IEC 61215 standard for 1000 and 2000 h. Tensile strength, thermal stability and degree of crosslinking have been found to be greater for PEVA containing lower VA content (18 and 24%) and diminished for PEVA with higher VA content (33 and 40%) due to DH aging.", "fact_category": "methodology", "relevance_score": 0.92, "confidence": 0.9, "perspective": "Methodological", "entities": ["PEVA", "vinyl acetate", "IEC 61215", "damp-heat aging", "85°C", "85% RH"]}, {"statement": "Tensile strength, thermal stability, and degree of crosslinking were found to be greater for PEVA containing lower VA content (18% and 24%) and diminished for PEVA with higher VA content (33% and 40%) after damp-heat aging, indicating that lower VA content improves mechanical resilience under humid thermal stress.", "direct_quote": "Tensile strength, thermal stability and degree of crosslinking have been found to be greater for PEVA containing lower VA content (18 and 24%) and diminished for PEVA with higher VA content (33 and 40%) due to DH aging. However, inadequate % transmittance and relatively high stiffness after 2000 h of DH aging discourages PEVA18 as an encapsulant. Moreover, PEVAs with higher VA content (33 and 40%) have been degraded significantly after 2000 h of DH aging", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.88, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["tensile strength", "thermal stability", "crosslinking", "VA content", "PEVA"]}, {"statement": "The PV industry consensus specifies that PEVA encapsulant should have VA content between 28% and 33%, but this study found that reducing VA content to 24% provides better encapsulation performance against damp-heat aging, encouraging the industry to adopt lower VA formulations.", "direct_quote": "According to the general consensus within the PV industry, PEVA as an encapsulant should have VA content between 28 and 33%. Degradation of PEVA films due to environmental stressors like humidity, temperature and solar radiation in the field aged modules is not uncommon. Moreover, degradation of PEVA encapsulant implies a concomitant loss in the electrical performance of the PV modules. VA content modulation especially outside the most commonly used and accepted range has been tried in this study to improve the damp heat aging resistance", "fact_category": "standard_reference", "relevance_score": 0.82, "confidence": 0.85, "perspective": "Industry", "entities": ["PV industry", "VA content", "28-33%", "encapsulant", "damp heat aging resistance"]}], "evidence_summary": "Study on modulating vinyl acetate content in PEVA encapsulants to improve damp-heat aging resistance. Found lower VA content (24%) improves tensile strength and crosslinking retention after 85°C/85%RH aging per IEC 61215. Directly relevant to encapsulant adhesion degradation under humid thermal stress."}]}
